---
title: "Contact"
description: "Accessible contact page for email and academic profiles."
lang: en
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
---

::: {.page-hero}
::: {.page-hero__content}
<p class="page-hero__eyebrow">Contact</p>

# Contact and Academic Profiles

<p class="page-hero__meta">Institutional email, research profiles, and a secure static contact approach</p>
<p class="page-hero__lede">
  Instead of using a backend contact form, this site adopts a secure and low-maintenance
  static contact model. The preferred approach is to keep institutional email and
  scholarly profile links together on one clear professional page.
</p>
:::
:::

::: {.highlight-grid}
::: {.highlight-card}
<div class="card-icon card-icon-muted" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-send-check"></i></div>

<p class="card-title">Preferred Contact</p>

Direct email is the most reliable channel for collaborations, speaking invitations,
and publication-related correspondence.
:::

::: {.highlight-card}
<div class="card-icon card-icon-muted" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-shield-check"></i></div>

<p class="card-title">Static and Secure</p>

The page avoids server-side forms, reduces maintenance, and does not unnecessarily increase spam exposure.
:::
:::

::: {.contact-shell}
::: {.section-heading}
<p class="section-kicker">Contact Channels</p>

## Direct Links {#contact-title}

<p class="section-lede">
  Email, ORCID, Google Scholar, ResearchGate, and GitHub are accessible at a glance.
</p>
:::

<ul class="contact-list">
  <li class="content-card">
    <div class="card-icon card-icon-accent" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i></div>
    <h3>Email</h3>
    <p>Primary channel for institutional and academic communication.</p>
    <p>
      <a class="contact-link icon-link" href="mailto:ad.soyad@kurum.edu.tr"><span>Write to the institutional email address</span><i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right" aria-hidden="true"></i></a>
    </p>
  </li>
  <li class="content-card">
    <div class="card-icon card-icon-accent" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-patch-check"></i></div>
    <h3>ORCID</h3>
    <p>Present your researcher identity and publication record in a standardized way.</p>
    <p>
      <a class="contact-link icon-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000"><span>View ORCID profile</span><i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right" aria-hidden="true"></i></a>
    </p>
  </li>
  <li class="content-card">
    <div class="card-icon card-icon-accent" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i></div>
    <h3>Google Scholar</h3>
    <p>One of the most practical academic indexes for citation visibility and publication scanning.</p>
    <p>
      <a class="contact-link icon-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/"><span>Open Google Scholar profile</span><i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right" aria-hidden="true"></i></a>
    </p>
  </li>
  <li class="content-card">
    <div class="card-icon card-icon-accent" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-people"></i></div>
    <h3>ResearchGate</h3>
    <p>An additional channel for networked visibility and shareable publication presence.</p>
    <p>
      <a class="contact-link icon-link" href="https://www.researchgate.net/"><span>View ResearchGate profile</span><i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right" aria-hidden="true"></i></a>
    </p>
  </li>
  <li class="content-card">
    <div class="card-icon card-icon-accent" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></div>
    <h3>GitHub</h3>
    <p>A good home for code, data, static site infrastructure, and open research outputs.</p>
    <p>
      <a class="contact-link icon-link" href="https://github.com/mdogus"><span>Open GitHub profile</span><i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right" aria-hidden="true"></i></a>
    </p>
  </li>
</ul>
:::

::: {.section-card}
## Additional Academic Profiles

You can also add links such as:

- Academia.edu
- LinkedIn
- SSRN
- Institutional staff page
:::

::: {.section-card}
## Contact Note

If you prefer not to display your email address openly, you can keep only institutional
profile links on this page or use a minimal non-visual obfuscation approach.
:::
